title: Chase Credit is Horrible
review: Most of the credit cards I have are for $8000 and up. Chase started me out with a $3500 credit card and then lowered my limit to $1500 despite the fact that I rarely keep a balance and never had any late payments. They have not increased my credit line despite the fact I have an amazing credit score! I recently applied for the Amazon Prime card and was NOT approved which seems crazy because I’m a long time Chase customer & other backs have recently given me upwards of 16K in credit (that I don’t spend because I use my credit to simply build more)… I find Chase to be the least appealing bank that has given me the least amount of available credit and it is also the ONLY bank that has ever reduced my credit limit! I’m actually quite OFFENDED and don’t think I would ever use them as my first interest in anything because of how I was treated as a person with great credit that has never missed or made any late payments.

review:
We appreciate the convenience and simplicity of using our Chase Sapphire Reserve card.
We use our card specifically for Travel. It is unfortunate that Chase decided to reduce the points earned for travel related changes from 3 points per dollar spent to 1 point per dollar spent.

We would be extremely grateful to Chase if they would go back to 3 points earned per dollar spent on travel expenses.

T-Mobile's native IPv6 architecture (utilizing 464XLAT translation) is frequently misidentified by Chase’s security systems as a proxy or an insecure connection. This often triggers an automatic account lockout after just two or three failed handshake attempts, causing major disruptions for business users relying on the T-Mobile network.
